instruction:
Design the complete technical architecture for "Sovereign-Node-Watch" - an AI-driven server monitoring and auto-remediation system for Hetzner customers.

ARCHITECTURE REQUIREMENTS:
1. System Components:
   - Lightweight agent running on customer servers (resource footprint < 50MB RAM)
   - Central dashboard/API for data aggregation and analysis
   - Alert pipeline (email, Slack, webhooks)
   - AI anomaly detection engine
   - Auto-remediation execution engine with safety guardrails

2. Hetzner Integration:
   - Which Hetzner APIs to leverage (Cloud API, Robot API, Network API)
   - How to authenticate and manage customer API keys securely
   - Server provisioning/scaling automation possibilities

3. Metrics Collection:
   - System metrics: CPU, RAM, disk usage, network I/O, load averages
   - Process monitoring: detect crashed services, resource hogs
   - Application-specific metrics (if possible to auto-detect)
   - Cost tracking: relate usage to Hetzner billing

4. AI/ML Approach:
   - Anomaly detection algorithm (statistical + machine learning)
   - Baseline establishment for new servers
   - Trend analysis for predictive alerts
   - User behavior learning (what actions do they typically take?)

5. Auto-Remediation Playbooks:
   - Disk cleanup (logs, temp files, package cache)
   - Service restart procedures
   - Resource scaling recommendations
   - Security hardening suggestions
   - Emergency failsafe: never auto-delete data or shut down servers

6. Data Architecture:
   - How to store time-series metrics efficiently
   - Customer data isolation and security
   - GDPR compliance considerations (EU customers)

7. Deployment Strategy:
   - How customers install the agent (script, package manager, Docker)
   - Update mechanism for agent software
   - Rollback procedures if updates fail

Save the complete architecture specification to shared/results/architecture_node_watch_v2.md with detailed technical diagrams (ASCII art is fine), component descriptions, and implementation recommendations.
